person.name: Roger le Bigod
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Roger le Bigod, Knt., of Settrington, Yorkshire, was the younger son of John le Bigod, Knt., by his second wife, Isabel _______. His date and place of birth are unknown and are estimated.

Roger married Joan _______. The date and place of their marriage are unknown and are estimated. 

«b»They had one son and two daughters:«/b»
1.) John, Knt., born about 1332-1334, died 13 November 1388, married Amy (or Anne) _______ and had issue.

2.) Isabel, wife of Roger de Burton, Knt. and Walter de Fauconberge, Knt., 4th Lord Fauconberge

3.) Joan, wife of William de Chauncy, Knt., of Skirpenbeck Yorkshire

In 1333, Roger was heir to his older brother, John le Bigod, Knt., inheriting manors in Yorkshire. In 1351, the King pardoned Roger for his felonies and trespasses which happened while rescuing Thomas Berer.

Sir Roger le Bigod died 17 April 1362.

zh.North Yorkshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger le Bigod於大約1292年出生在英國時，他的父親John le Bigod49歲，母親Isabel28歲。他於1329年與Joan在英國越可爾澤特林頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1362年4月17日在英國越可爾澤特林頓享年71歲，埋葬在英國越可爾澤特林頓。

zh-hans.North Yorkshire, England: 英国英格兰諾斯 越可爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger le Bigod于大约1292年出生在英国时，他的父亲John le Bigod49岁，母亲Isabel28岁。他于1329年与Joan在英国越可爾澤特林頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1362年4月17日在英国越可爾澤特林頓享年71岁，埋葬在英国越可爾澤特林頓。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Bigod
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jaroslaw, Jozef, Krystyna, Slawomir.

Polish and Jewish (from Poland): from bigos, formerlydenoting anything that was chopped or slashed, now a particular dishcomposed of hash and sauerkraut; also ‘confusion’; hence perhaps anickname for someone who caused confusion.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger le Bigod was born about 1292, in England, his father, John le Bigod, was 49 and his mother, Isabel, was 28. He married Joan in 1329, in Settrington, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 17 April 1362, in Settrington, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 71, and was buried in Settrington, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger le Bigod est né environ 1292, à Angleterre, son père, John le Bigod, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Isabel, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Joan en 1329, à Settrington, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 17 avril 1362, à Settrington, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Settrington, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger le Bigod um 1292 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John le Bigod 49 und seine Mutter Isabel 28. Er hat 1329 in Settrington, Yorkshire, England Joan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 17. April 1362 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Settrington, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Settrington, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger le Bigod è nato il circa 1292 nel England, il suo padre, John le Bigod, aveva 49 anni e il suo madre, Isabel, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Joan nel 1329, nel Settrington, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 17 aprile 1362, nel Settrington, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolto a Settrington, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger le Bigodが約1292年にイギリスで生まれた時，父John le Bigodは49歳，母Isabelは28歳であった。1329年にイギリスヨークシュアイアセットリントンでJoanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1362年4月17日にイギリスヨークシュアイアセットリントンで71歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアセットリントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger le Bigod(이)가 대략 1292년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John le Bigod(은)는 49세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1329년 영국 요르크실르 세트링턴에서 Joan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1362년 4월 17일 영국 요르크실르 세트링턴에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 세트링턴에 묻혔다.

mn.North Yorkshire, England: North Yorkshire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger le Bigod nasceu aproximadamente 1292, em Inglaterra, seu pai, John le Bigod, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Isabel, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan em 1329, em Settrington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 17 de abril de 1362, em Settrington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Settrington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger le Bigod родился около 1292 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени John le Bigod было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan в 1329 г. в местности сеттрингтон, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 17 апреля 1362 г. в местности сеттрингтон, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности сеттрингтон, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger le Bigod nació aproximadamente 1292, en Inglaterra, su papá, John le Bigod, tenía 49 años y su mamá, Isabel, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Joan en 1329, en Settrington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 17 de abril de 1362, en Settrington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Settrington, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
